document.name: playground-ui-ux-contract
document.maintenance_rule: Update this file whenever new UI elements are added, removed, renamed, or behaviorally changed.

primary_jobs: load a local sprite sheet or frame folder

regions.position: left on desktop, top on mobile
right on desktop, bottom on mobile

sections.title: Asset
sections.purpose: load and validate a local sprite sheet or frame sequence

elements.label: Mode
elements.behavior: switches the asset loading mode and updates the picker and grid availability

elements.label: File
elements.behavior: loads a local image or a selected folder of frame images and initializes preview runtime

elements.label: Select image / Select folder
elements.behavior: changes with the selected asset mode

elements.label: Asset metadata
elements.behavior: shows image size or load error

sections.title: Grid
sections.purpose: define the regular sprite sheet grid

sections.title: Timing
behavior_notes: If only fps is set, playback advances frame-by-frame.
If only duration is set, frames are resolved by overall progress.
If both are set, duration has priority and frame skipping is allowed.

sections.title: Transform
sections.purpose: position and scale the rendered animation

sections.title: Playback
sections.purpose: control animation state and inspect runtime status

elements.label: State
elements.behavior: shows idle, playing, paused, or stopped

sections.title: Config
sections.purpose: export current runtime settings as JSON

sections.title: Preview
sections.purpose: display the canvas output of the current runtime configuration

elements.label: Preview message
elements.behavior: communicates current preview state or action result

elements.label: Preview canvas
elements.behavior: renders current frame using runtime player and canvas renderer

interaction_rules: Changing grid, timing, transform, or canvas inputs immediately recalculates the runtime config through Vue reactive state.
Selecting sprite-sheet mode loads a single image and starts playback.
Selecting frame-sequence mode loads a folder of frame images sorted by relative path or file name and starts playback.
Grid controls are disabled while frame-sequence mode is active.
Pause preserves the current frame state; stop resets to the initial frame.
JSON output always mirrors the current UI state.
Save JSON downloads the current config; Copy JSON writes it to clipboard when permitted.
implementation_notes: The playground UI is implemented in Vue 3 using Composition API.
Canvas preview and asset orchestration live in a dedicated composable/runtime context instead of imperative DOM bindings.
Sidebar and preview components read shared state from the runtime composable instead of using prop drilling through App.
The Vue layer is internal to the playground and does not change the framework-agnostic public runtime API.

responsive_behavior.desktop: two-column layout with controls on the left and preview on the right
responsive_behavior.tablet: single-column layout with preview under controls
responsive_behavior.mobile: single-column layout and one-column section grids
